videos.title: Hindi | 11th May, Monday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi | 11th May, Monday - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

videos.title: Struggling With Your 10-2  Try This Drill!
videos.summary_source_text:
Struggling With Your 10-2  Try This Drill! The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: DOUBLE SWEEP! Carolina Hurricanes Sweep Their Way to the Eastern Conference Final!
videos.summary_source_text:
DOUBLE SWEEP! Carolina Hurricanes Sweep Their Way to the Eastern Conference Final! The Carolina Hurricanes punched their ticket to the Eastern Conference Final for the second straight playoffs, and third time in four seasons. What stood out in Game 4 and Round 2 as the Hurricanes' stay undefeated through two rounds of the playoffs. On the Monday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on how the Hurricanes punched their ticket to their third Eastern Conference Final in four seasons. Furthermore, Zach discusses how the Canes joined some elite company, going 8-0 through the first two rounds and holding teams to 10 goals through eight games. Secondly, Zach talks about who stood out the most not only in Game 4, but the series as a whole. Who was the series MVP for the Hurricanes? Finally, Zach goes over why the Mark Jankowski extension is a win-win for both sides. What could this mean moving forward? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: Grab The Ice For A Harder Shot
videos.summary_source_text:
Grab The Ice For A Harder Shot "Hands out and stay there." 
That's the cue that's quietly killing your shot.
If your top hand stays extended, you're stiff, you're pushing the puck instead of using leverage from the bounce and spring of your muscles and that stick flex everyone's chasing never actually shows up.

Here's the fix, these are the three steps modern NHL goal scorers use:

✅ Use the bounce and spring of your muscles by pulling the puck to the "sweet spot" and releasing using the elastic springs of your muscles.

✅ Keep your blade mostly in place while both hands move toward the net.

✅ When your hands are extended → pull your top hand into your body, chest, and then back to the crevice.

That pull-in is what grabs the ice. That's where the leverage comes from and when leverage is in the sweet spot, the flex happens on its own.

This is core Slingshot Shooting System mechanics which are reverse-engineered from how the world's best shooters generate their power.

🔗 Want the full system? Check out Train 2.0+ for complete access to the Slingshot Shooting System and the Downhill Skating System.

Sam Altman called it "the biggest thing we've ever done as a 
company." Jony Ive said it should feel like "a cabin by a lake." 
Court filings confirm it ships in early 2027. But nobody — not 
even OpenAI's own employees — seems able to describe what this 
device actually IS.

No screen. Not glasses. Not a watch. Not earbuds.

In this video, I break down EVERYTHING we know about OpenAI's 
mystery device — the confirmed timeline, the leaked form factors, 
the rumored price, and the one detail buried in a sworn legal 
filing that tells you exactly when this thing is launching.

leaks, and every credible report from WSJ, The Information, 
The Verge, and Wired to piece this together. What I found is 
way bigger than the headlines are letting on.

videos.title: Stop Waking Up to Pee at Night. Do This Standing | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
Stop Waking Up to Pee at Night. Do This Standing | Dr. Mandell Are you waking up multiple times every night to urinate and wondering why it keeps happening? In this video, I’ll show you a simple standing movement that may help relax tension deep within the pelvis, hips, lower back, and pelvic floor muscles that surround and support the bladder. Many people don’t realize that sitting too much, poor posture, stress, tight hip flexors, weak glutes, pelvic tension, and restricted movement patterns can all place added pressure on the bladder and surrounding nerves. This gentle pelvic movement may help improve mobility, activate supporting muscles, reduce stiffness, and promote relaxation throughout the lower pelvis and lower abdomen. It may also help people dealing with pelvic tightness, nighttime urinary urgency, prostatitis symptoms, pelvic floor tension, hip stiffness, lower back discomfort, constipation, and stress-related tightness throughout the body. The best part is that it’s simple, easy to follow, and many people feel changes almost immediately. Try this movement slowly before bed tonight and pay attention to how your body feels overnight and the next morning. Your body may simply be holding onto more tension than you realize.

*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

videos.title: The BEST and WORST Cardio for Fat Loss (SURPRISE!)
videos.summary_source_text:
The BEST and WORST Cardio for Fat Loss (SURPRISE!) If you are doing cardio to lose fat, there is a good chance you are making the worst cardio mistakes everyone makes for fat loss without even knowing it. In this video, I am going to show you why the treadmill, elliptical, StairMaster, bike, and even your fitness tracker may be lying to you about how many calories you burned. If you use those numbers to decide how much more you can eat, you could be wiping out your calorie deficit.

Avoid These Cardio Mistakes Here - https://athleanx.com
Subscribe to This Channel Here - http://bit.ly/2b0coMW

The first cardio mistake for fat loss is using cardio as the main way to create your calorie deficit. Cardio can help you lose weight, but it should not be the foundation of your fat loss plan. You cannot outrun a bad diet. One bad meal, snack, or portion mistake can erase an hour of cardio fast. If you want to lose fat and keep it off, clean up your nutrition first and use cardio to support those efforts.

The next mistake is trusting the calorie number on the machine. Most cardio machines estimate calories using METs, or metabolic equivalents. Many estimates are based on one standard body weight, often around 154 pounds. If you weigh more or less than that, the number on the screen is already wrong. If the machine does not ask for your body weight, do not trust the calorie count.

Another mistake is not realizing that some machines may count calories you would have burned anyway. Your resting energy expenditure is the energy you burn sitting around doing nothing. If a treadmill says you burned 420 calories, that may not mean you burned 420 extra calories from the workout. Part of that number may include your resting burn, which means you are overestimating your true exercise calories.

Leaning on the machine is one of the biggest cardio mistakes people make. Holding onto the treadmill, leaning over the bike, hanging on the StairMaster, or unloading your body weight with your arms makes the exercise easier. Easier means less work and fewer calories burned. If your goal is fat loss, stand tall, keep your hands off the rails when possible, and make your body do the work.

Cutting your range of motion short also kills cardio results. On the stepmill or StairMaster, short, choppy steps reduce the work you do and prevent full hip extension. The elliptical can lock you into a shortened range of motion too. Just like a half rep curl is not as effective as a full rep, half reps on cardio are not giving you the best fat loss return.

Some cardio machines are more inaccurate than others. The stationary bike is usually one of the better options, especially when it uses watts and lets you enter your body weight. The StairMaster can be fairly close if you are not leaning on it. The treadmill can be off because of calibration issues. The elliptical is the worst offender because the calorie estimates can be way off.

If you think you burned 130 more calories than you actually did and eat those calories back every day, you can erase your deficit. Over time, that mistake can be the difference between losing fat and gaining weight.

If you want better cardio for fat loss, choose machines that make you do more total work. The air bike, rowing machine, and ski erg are great options because they involve both the upper and lower body with real effort. They are harder, and that is the point. The more muscles you involve, the more work you do, and the more calories you burn.

If you still want to use your favorite cardio machine, use one that allows you to enter your body weight. Then stop staring at the calorie display. Throw a towel over the screen and judge your effort by your breathing. If you can easily carry on a conversation, you are probably not working hard enough. If your breathing is challenged, your effort and calorie burn are likely higher.

A simple rule is to take the calorie number the machine gives you and cut it in half. Do not assume your fitness watch fixes the problem either. Wearables can be inaccurate too, so use them for trends, not as a precise calorie calculator.

If you are looking for a complete step by step training and nutrition plan designed to help you build muscle, burn fat, and look your best, be sure to head to athleanx.com and use the program selector tool to find the plan that matches your goals.

For more videos on how to lose fat, build muscle, and train smarter without wasting time on common workout mistakes, be sure to subscribe to our channel here on youtube via the link above and remember to turn on your notifications. The StairMaster can be fairly close if you are not leaning on it. The treadmill can be off because of calibration issues. The elliptical is the worst offender because the calorie estimates can be way off.

If you think you burned 130 more calories than you actually did and eat those calories back every day, you can erase your deficit. Over time, that mistake can be the difference between losing fat and gaining weight.

If you want better cardio for fat loss, choose machines that make you do more total work. The air bike, rowing machine, and ski erg are great options because they involve both the upper and lower body with real effort. They are harder, and that is the point. The more muscles you involve, the more work you do, and the more calories you burn.

If you still want to use your favorite cardio machine, use one that allows you to enter your body weight. Then stop staring at the calorie display. Throw a towel over the screen and judge your effort by your breathing. If you can easily carry on a conversation, you are probably not working hard enough. If your breathing is challenged, your effort and calorie burn are likely higher.

A simple rule is to take the calorie number the machine gives you and cut it in half. Do not assume your fitness watch fixes the problem either. Wearables can be inaccurate too, so use them for trends, not as a precise calorie calculator.

If you are looking for a complete step by step training and nutrition plan designed to help you build muscle, burn fat, and look your best, be sure to head to athleanx.com and use the program selector tool to find the plan that matches your goals.

For more videos on how to lose fat, build muscle, and train smarter without wasting time on common workout mistakes, be sure to subscribe to our channel here on youtube via the link above and remember to turn on your notifications.
videos.summary: The BEST and WORST Cardio for Fat Loss (SURPRISE!) If you are doing cardio to lose fat, there is a good chance you are making the worst cardio mistakes everyone makes for fat loss without even knowing it. In this video, I am going to show you why the treadmill, elliptical, StairMaster, bike, and even your fitness tracker may be lying to you about how many calories you burned.

videos.title: Is This The Best Value Putter In Golf Right Now?
videos.summary_source_text:
Is This The Best Value Putter In Golf Right Now? Rick gets his hands on the Wilson Infinite ZT putters,  a face-balanced, zero-twist design that promises tour-level performance for £200. But do they actually deliver, or is the price tag the most impressive thing about them? Watch to find out.

Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: The Greatest Formula 1 Rivalries | F1 Nation Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
The Greatest Formula 1 Rivalries | F1 Nation Podcast With an intra-team title battle brewing between Kimi Antonelli and George Russell at Mercedes in 2026, Tom Clarkson is joined by Jolyon Palmer and James Hinchcliffe to chat about some of the greatest teammate rivalries in F1 history.

The guys discuss Lewis Hamilton’s battles with Nico Rosberg at Mercedes and Fernando Alonso at McLaren, the major flashpoints during Sebastian Vettel and Mark Webber’s time at Red Bull, and the politics between Ayrton Senna and Alain Prost at McLaren. 

Why were those rivalries so fierce? What are the key ingredients for an intense fight between teammates? How much involvement should the team have? And of the current grid, which driver pairing do Tom, Jolyon and Hinch think would have the most heated rivalry if they were competing for a title?

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:
https://www.youtube.com/@LIVGolf

videos.title: AI Is Being Built Into New Homes
videos.summary_source_text:
AI Is Being Built Into New Homes Your future home might come with an AI supercomputer built into the wall.

NVIDIA, Pulte Group, and startup SPAN are working on homes with built-in mini data centers packed with Blackwell GPUs, AMD CPUs, and terabytes of RAM.

So in the future, your house could potentially run your own personal AI locally and maybe even rent out unused compute power to companies like OpenAI or Anthropic when you’re not using it.

Would you want this in your home?

videos.title: Perfect Your Takeaway And Wrist Hinge In Under 6 Minutes
videos.summary_source_text:
Perfect Your Takeaway And Wrist Hinge In Under 6 Minutes Perfect your takeaway and wrist hinge — too much arm rotation is destroying your swing in the first foot. Eric Cogorno's 2 o'clock fix changes everything.

🏌️ WORK WITH ERIC COGORNO IN PERSON! Two-Day Golf Schools in Florida and Pennsylvania - Limited Spots Available!
📞 Call or text us at ‪(610) 756-7730‬ to discuss if our golf schools are a good fit for you. ‬‬
👉 Register Here: https://bit.ly/48bbScn

📱 GET THE SWING COACH APP The BEST Golf Swing Training App Ever Created
👉 Download Here: https://inf.getswingcoach.com/EC1

🎯 PERSONALIZED ONLINE COACHING Get Custom Golf Instruction from Eric Cogorno
👉 Learn More: https://www.cogornogolf.com

📚 STEP-BY-STEP TRAINING PROGRAMS Eric's Complete Golf Improvement System
👉 Start Here: https://ericcogornogolf.podia.com

🛒 RECOMMENDED GOLF EQUIPMENT & TRAINING AIDS:
Takomo Golf - https://takomogolf.com
Precision Impact Right Wrist Trainer - https://pureswingproducts.com/?sca_ref=1815522.bXQUsGp2bS (Code: COGORNO for $20 OFF)
theHanger Training Aid - https://www.hangergolf.com/?sca_ref=944104.HqM9GYcX2I (Code: COGORNO for 15% OFF)

⏰ TIMESTAMPS:
🏌️ 0:00 Eric Cogorno: Why 80% of Golfers Destroy Their Swing in the First Foot
⛳ 0:29 The Hideki Model — Hands Straight Back, Club Down the Toe Line
🎯 1:08 Why a Correct Takeaway Sets Up Your Entire Golf Swing
💥 1:37 The #1 Takeaway Mistake: Why 80% of Golfers Go Too Far Inside
⚡ 2:12 The Secret to a Great Takeaway — The 2 O'Clock Feeling
🔥 2:42 Two Root Causes: Too Much Arm Rotation & Not Enough Wrist Hinge
✅ 3:10 The Other Common Error: Hands and Club Going Out
🏆 4:06 The Only Takeaway Drill You'll Ever Need (Two Rehearsals, One Hit)
🚀 5:12 The 30-Day Challenge That Will Transform Your Takeaway

In This Video, You'll Discover:
•Takeaway mastery using the exact model Eric breaks down — hands straight back, club right down the toe line, toe in front of the heel
•Why wrist hinge in the golf swing is one of the two non-negotiable keys to staying on plane early — and how most golfers unknowingly eliminate it
•The two most common golf swing faults in the takeaway (too far inside AND too far outside) and the single fix that works for both
•A simple 2 o'clock visual that immediately corrects the club path in your golf drills practice — no guesswork, instant feedback
•How to use the two-rehearsal, one-hit drill with the Swing Coach app or video to lock in a correct, on-plane takeaway for good
•The 30-day half-swing protocol that Eric Cogorno uses with students to permanently rewire the start of the golf swing
•Why rushing to full swing too fast is the reason most golf lessons and range sessions don't stick — and how to avoid that trap

What separates this from a generic takeaway tip is the precision of the prescription: 20 reps, three times a week, half swings only, for a full 30 days — nothing longer until the pattern is locked in. Eric Cogorno built this protocol around the two root causes he sees in 80% of the golfers he works with: excess arm rotation and insufficient wrist hinge in the golf swing, both of which are addressed simultaneously by the 2 o'clock drill. Use the Swing Coach app or down-the-line video to confirm the shaft is riding the toe line on every rep — that feedback loop is what makes the 30-day commitment actually work.

videos.title: China is WINNING the luxury car BATTLE!
videos.summary_source_text:
China is WINNING the luxury car BATTLE! Thanks to everyone who lent us their cars for this video: 
- Zeekr 8X: https://www.instagram.com/kainiansgarage/ 
- Rolls-Royce Cullinan: https://www.instagram.com/seanysgarage

It’s time for a China vs Britain luxury showdown! 

Mat’s got his hands on the range-topping Zeekr 8X, and he’s going to compare it to a 5-year-old, second-hand Rolls-Royce Cullinan to see if massive tech can truly unseat the king of luxury SUVs. 

If you’re looking solely for performance, it’s easy to see why you may choose the Zeekr. It may only be equipped with a 2-litre 4-cylinder turbo petrol engine, but this is used to power three electric motors which combine to produce a staggering 1,400hp and 1,410Nm of torque!

In comparison, the Cullinan may have a huge 6.75-litre twin-turbo V12, but this can ‘only’ produce 571hp and 850Nm. However, the Rolls-Royce is the lighter of the two at 2,660kg, whereas the Zeekr’s heavy battery tech pushes it up to 2,820 kg!

But chances are, if you’re in the market for one of these two cars, luxury tech and quality features are likely to be top of your agenda, and both of these cars are stacked full of them. Both cars come with soft-close automatic doors, massage seats in the front (although the Zeekr has them in the rear as well), and large infotainment screens in the front. However, while the interiors of both cars are styled relatively well, the Rolls easily wins it thanks to the quality of the materials throughout the cabin. Almost any element you touch feels expensive - but what else would you expect from a Rolls-Royce!

However, there’s one area where the Zeekrs wins hands down - the price. The Rolls-Royce we have here, despite being 5 years old and second-hand, still costs £250,000. As for the Zeekr? As new, it only costs £54,300! 

So will Mat prefer the Zeekr to the Rolls-Royce? You’ll need to keep watching to see for yourself!

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-1105

videos.title: Tiger Woods Record Broken | Every Shot From Yurav Premlall’s Winning Back Nine
videos.summary_source_text:
Tiger Woods Record Broken | Every Shot From Yurav Premlall’s Winning Back Nine Rewatch Yurav Premlall's Winning Back 9 at the Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship in the Back 9 on Sunday presented by Nexo. Premlall stormed to a stunning 14-shot victory as he claimed his maiden DP World Tour title surpassing Tiger Woods' previous record winning margin of 12 shots for a maiden title on the DP World Tour.

► SUBSCRIBE: http://et.golf/Subscribe 

The DP World Tour is back to where it all started this week. Spain was the host of the first ever DP World Tour - then European Tour - event in 1972 so it is fitting that it is where the 2026 European Swing gets started. The fourth of five Global Swings on the 2026 Race to Dubai, the European Swing consists of six counting events - four of which are national opens - with the US PGA Championship and U.S. Open also happening within the same timeframe. Spain has long been one of the world's great golfing nations and this week local heroes will take centre stage, with a string of established and rising stars searching for home glory. Nacho Elvira has already won in 2026 and will be hungry for more, as will fellow winners in recent seasons Eugenio Chacarra, Alejandro del Rey, Angel Hidalgo and Pablo Larrazábal.

Welcome to the Official YouTube channel of the DP World Tour. Every week you can find extended tournament highlights and individual player highlights from the likes of Rory McIlroy, Justin Rose and Tommy Fleetwood. This is also the home of the 14 Club Challenge, Little Interviews, Fastest Hole and many more challenges, pranks and world record attempts featuring the biggest golfers in the world.

videos.title: 465. Finansbranschens tre stora problem — med Per Palmström
videos.summary_source_text:
465. Finansbranschens tre stora problem — med Per Palmström Tre problem i svensk finansrådgivning som Finansinspektionen själv har dokumenterat men ingenting har hänt. Per Palmström och Jan Bolmeson om vad som är fel och varför.

Jan hade nyligen tre minuter med statsminister Ulf Kristersson och EU:s talman Roberta Metsola. Det här avsnittet är vad han ville säga — baserat på offentlig data från Finansinspektionen, Mercer och SCB.

Per Palmström (jurist och oberoende finansiell rådgivare, medgrundare på Evida) går igenom tre konkreta problem:

▸ Incitamentsproblem i rådgivningen - Storbankskunder betalar i snitt 1 % i fondavgift, mot 0,4 % hos  nätmäklare. 80 % av storbankskunderna har en aktivt förvaltad fond, mot 50 % hos nätmäklarna. Skillnaden är inte slumpmässig.

▸ Försäkringskryphålet i tjänstepensionen - Av 4 600 miljarder i tjänstepensioner ligger 2 500 miljarder i traditionell försäkring — ofta med flera procentenheter lägre avkastning än en bred, billig global indexfond.

▸ Finansiell utbildning saknas - Svensk grundskola ägnar ungefär lika mycket tid åt privatekonomi 
   som åt att rensa fisk. FI rekommenderade ett totalt provisionsförbud 2016 — tio år senare har ingenting hänt.

══════════════════════

KAPITEL

00:00:00 - Introduktion till avsnittet
00:03:33 - Vad som är bra i det svenska finanssystemet – positiv start
00:05:03 - Välfärdsstaten minskar behovet av privatsparande
00:06:00 - Per om sin stroke och livsperspektiv ett år senare
00:09:51 - ISK och Sveriges gynnsamma kapitalskatt
00:14:21 - Pensionssystemet internationellt, fondkultur och finansiell stabilitet
00:19:12 - Bakgrunden: mötet med statsministern och EU-talmanen
00:21:09 - Incitamentproblemet: banken tjänar mer när du väljer sämre produkter
00:25:45 - FI-rapport 2025: storbankerna har dyrare fonder och högre andel aktivt förvaltade
00:28:48 - Per: banker borde inte äga fondbolag – intressekonflikten är oundviklig
00:31:00 - Provisionsdriven rådgivning ger lägre avkastning – äldre sparare betalar mest
00:34:18 - Helkund på storbank: fondavgifterna överstiger bolånerabatten
00:37:21 - Storbankernas 213 miljarder i vinst och räntehöjningar utan Riksbanksbeslut
00:41:33 - Konkurrens, inlåsningseffekter och BankID-beroende
00:46:45 - FI kallar det ett marknadsmisslyckande – ingenting har hänt sedan 2016
00:49:33 - Oberoende rådgivning: svårt att hitta och dyrt att driva
00:54:15 - Tre vanliga spararfrågor – och varför lagstiftningen gör det svårt att svara
01:01:09 - Vad finansiell rådgivning kostar i tid, dokumentation och startkapital
01:06:00 - Försäkringsförmedlare som strukturellt kryphål: 99% provision
01:10:39 - Tjänstepensionerna: 4 600 miljarder och varför traditionell försäkring dominerar
01:13:00 - Traditionell försäkring: paternalistisk modell och lägre avkastning
01:18:45 - HR väljer pensionslösning efter bekvämlighet, inte pensionsnytta
01:22:12 - Vad som behöver förändras: kunskap och incitamentstrukturer
01:23:27 - Finansiell utbildning i skolan – hemkunskapen hinner inte med privatekonomi
01:27:09 - Provisionsförbud i andra länder och bättre riskinformation till sparare
01:30:00 - Stjärnförvaltarmyten: Buffetts avkastning förklarad av riskfaktorer
01:33:09 - Premiepensionen och fondtorgsnämndens begränsningar
01:36:21 - AP7 Såfa-effekten: tre procentenheter bättre avkastning för de som stannar
01:39:00 - Lobbyverksamhet och personliga nätverk i finansbranschen
01:40:12 - Sammanfattning: ta bort intressekonflikter och höj den finansiella kunskapen

══════════════════════

▸ Incitamentsproblem i rådgivningen - Storbankskunder betalar i snitt 1 % i fondavgift, mot 0,4 % hos  nätmäklare. 80 % av storbankskunderna har en aktivt förvaltad fond, mot 50 % hos nätmäklarna. Skillnaden är inte slumpmässig.

▸ Försäkringskryphålet i tjänstepensionen - Av 4 600 miljarder i tjänstepensioner ligger 2 500 miljarder i traditionell försäkring — ofta med flera procentenheter lägre avkastning än en bred, billig global indexfond.

▸ Finansiell utbildning saknas - Svensk grundskola ägnar ungefär lika mycket tid åt privatekonomi 
   som åt att rensa fisk. FI rekommenderade ett totalt provisionsförbud 2016 — tio år senare har ingenting hänt.

══════════════════════

KAPITEL

00:00:00 - Introduktion till avsnittet
00:03:33 - Vad som är bra i det svenska finanssystemet – positiv start
00:05:03 - Välfärdsstaten minskar behovet av privatsparande
00:06:00 - Per om sin stroke och livsperspektiv ett år senare
00:09:51 - ISK och Sveriges gynnsamma kapitalskatt
00:14:21 - Pensionssystemet internationellt, fondkultur och finansiell stabilitet
00:19:12 - Bakgrunden: mötet med statsministern och EU-talmanen
00:21:09 - Incitamentproblemet: banken tjänar mer när du väljer sämre produkter
00:25:45 - FI-rapport 2025: storbankerna har dyrare fonder och högre andel aktivt förvaltade
00:28:48 - Per: banker borde inte äga fondbolag – intressekonflikten är oundviklig
00:31:00 - Provisionsdriven rådgivning ger lägre avkastning – äldre sparare betalar mest
00:34:18 - Helkund på storbank: fondavgifterna överstiger bolånerabatten
00:37:21 - Storbankernas 213 miljarder i vinst och räntehöjningar utan Riksbanksbeslut
00:41:33 - Konkurrens, inlåsningseffekter och BankID-beroende
00:46:45 - FI kallar det ett marknadsmisslyckande – ingenting har hänt sedan 2016
00:49:33 - Oberoende rådgivning: svårt att hitta och dyrt att driva
00:54:15 - Tre vanliga spararfrågor – och varför lagstiftningen gör det svårt att svara
01:01:09 - Vad finansiell rådgivning kostar i tid, dokumentation och startkapital
01:06:00 - Försäkringsförmedlare som strukturellt kryphål: 99% provision
01:10:39 - Tjänstepensionerna: 4 600 miljarder och varför traditionell försäkring dominerar
01:13:00 - Traditionell försäkring: paternalistisk modell och lägre avkastning
01:18:45 - HR väljer pensionslösning efter bekvämlighet, inte pensionsnytta
01:22:12 - Vad som behöver förändras: kunskap och incitamentstrukturer
01:23:27 - Finansiell utbildning i skolan – hemkunskapen hinner inte med privatekonomi
01:27:09 - Provisionsförbud i andra länder och bättre riskinformation till sparare
01:30:00 - Stjärnförvaltarmyten: Buffetts avkastning förklarad av riskfaktorer
01:33:09 - Premiepensionen och fondtorgsnämndens begränsningar
01:36:21 - AP7 Såfa-effekten: tre procentenheter bättre avkastning för de som stannar
01:39:00 - Lobbyverksamhet och personliga nätverk i finansbranschen
01:40:12 - Sammanfattning: ta bort intressekonflikter och höj den finansiella kunskapen

videos.title: Slafkovsky's slick tip extends the lead on the power play 🇸🇰🚨
videos.summary_source_text:
Slafkovsky's slick tip extends the lead on the power play 🇸🇰🚨 Another Montreal power play, another Juraj Slafkovsky goal! The budding Slovak star redirects Lane Hutson's shot past the Sabres' Alex Lyon for his 4th goal of the playoffs - all of which have come on the power play - extending the Canadiens' lead to 4-1. With the goal, Slafkovsky becomes just the second Montreal player on record since 1934 to have each of his first four goals in a postseason be power-play goals, joining Dickie Moore (4 in 1958).

videos.summary_source_text:

Anaheim goes on to beat Vegas 3-2, tying the second-round series 2-2.

videos.title: PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 4 | Truist Championship | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 4 | Truist Championship | 2026 Check out the best shots of the day from Round 4 of the 2026 Truist Championship, featuring Kristoffer Reitan, Rickie Fowler, Alex Fitzpatrick and Nicolai Højgaard, among others.

The PGA TOUR heads to Quail Hollow Club in Charlotte, North Carolina, for the Truist Championship, one of the standout events on the calendar. Rory McIlroy returns to a venue where he has enjoyed incredible success and headlines a star-studded field. He is joined by a host of top talent including Cameron Young, Xander Schauffele, Justin Thomas, Tommy Fleetwood, Matt Fitzpatrick and Justin Rose, as players continue their push for FedExCup points during a crucial stretch of the season. Known for its demanding closing stretch, the “Green Mile,” Quail Hollow promises drama and excitement down the back nine. The New Agentic AI Workflow Feels Too Powerful AI agents are starting to move beyond single outputs. This new workflow turns rough ideas, sketches, prompts, images, and edits into full creative projects, from app screens and brand directions to cinematic videos, animated characters, and finished campaign-style assets.

How Abacus AI Agent turns rough ideas into full workflows

videos.title: Wrap Aluminum Foil Around Your Feet Before Bed… See What Happens | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
Wrap Aluminum Foil Around Your Feet Before Bed… See What Happens | Dr. Mandell Many people are trying one of the strangest bedtime rituals imaginable, wrapping aluminum foil around their feet before sleep. It may sound unusual at first, but once you understand the incredible connection between your feet, your nervous system, circulation, sensory receptors, body awareness, warmth, pressure stimulation, and the brain, this simple nighttime experiment becomes surprisingly fascinating. Your feet contain thousands of nerve endings that constantly send information back to the brain and spinal cord, influencing balance, comfort, muscle tension, circulation, and relaxation responses throughout the body. In this video, Dr. Mandell explains why certain types of sensory stimulation around the feet may create unusual sensations, why warmth and gentle pressure can affect the nervous system, and why so many people report waking up with feet that feel different after trying this strange bedtime trick. This is not about magic. It is about sensory input, relaxation, body awareness, and experiencing how your nervous system responds. Tonight, try this simple experiment for yourself and pay attention to how your feet, legs, and body feel the next morning. Sometimes the strangest wellness rituals become the most interesting conversations.

*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

3 eggs.
Like this video to save this healthy bread recipe and keep it safe.
1 cup (240 ml) kefir.
1/4 cup (35 g) psyllium husk.
1/2 cup (70 g) almonds. 1/3 cup (50 g) pumpkin seeds.
1/3 cup (40 g) sunflower seeds.
2 tbsp tomato flakes. Salt.
Olive oil.
Sesame seeds.
Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for about 1 hour.

videos.title: RECORD-BREAKING ROUND | 2026 Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship
videos.summary_source_text:
RECORD-BREAKING ROUND | 2026 Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship Every shot of Yurav Premlall's record breaking final round at the Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship where the South African stormed to a stunning 14-shot victory as he claimed his maiden DP World Tour title.

► SUBSCRIBE: http://et.golf/Subscribe 

The DP World Tour is back to where it all started this week. Spain was the host of the first ever DP World Tour - then European Tour - event in 1972 so it is fitting that it is where the 2026 European Swing gets started. The fourth of five Global Swings on the 2026 Race to Dubai, the European Swing consists of six counting events - four of which are national opens - with the US PGA Championship and U.S. Open also happening within the same timeframe. Spain has long been one of the world's great golfing nations and this week local heroes will take centre stage, with a string of established and rising stars searching for home glory. Nacho Elvira has already won in 2026 and will be hungry for more, as will fellow winners in recent seasons Eugenio Chacarra, Alejandro del Rey, Angel Hidalgo and Pablo Larrazábal.

Welcome to the Official YouTube channel of the DP World Tour. Every week you can find extended tournament highlights and individual player highlights from the likes of Rory McIlroy, Justin Rose and Tommy Fleetwood. This is also the home of the 14 Club Challenge, Little Interviews, Fastest Hole and many more challenges, pranks and world record attempts featuring the biggest golfers in the world. 

► WEBSITE: http://www.europeantour.com
► FACEBOOK: https://www.facebook.com/DPWorldTour
► TWITTER https://twitter.com/DPWorldTour
► INSTAGRAM https://www.instagram.com/dpworldtour/ Every shot of Yurav Premlall's record breaking final round at the Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship where the South African stormed to a stunning 14-shot victory as he claimed his maiden DP World Tour title.

► SUBSCRIBE: http://et.golf/Subscribe 

The DP World Tour is back to where it all started this week. Spain was the host of the first ever DP World Tour - then European Tour - event in 1972 so it is fitting that it is where the 2026 European Swing gets started. The fourth of five Global Swings on the 2026 Race to Dubai, the European Swing consists of six counting events - four of which are national opens - with the US PGA Championship and U.S. Open also happening within the same timeframe. Spain has long been one of the world's great golfing nations and this week local heroes will take centre stage, with a string of established and rising stars searching for home glory. Nacho Elvira has already won in 2026 and will be hungry for more, as will fellow winners in recent seasons Eugenio Chacarra, Alejandro del Rey, Angel Hidalgo and Pablo Larrazábal.

Welcome to the Official YouTube channel of the DP World Tour. Every week you can find extended tournament highlights and individual player highlights from the likes of Rory McIlroy, Justin Rose and Tommy Fleetwood. This is also the home of the 14 Club Challenge, Little Interviews, Fastest Hole and many more challenges, pranks and world record attempts featuring the biggest golfers in the world. 

► WEBSITE: http://www.europeantour.com

► TWITTER https://twitter.com/DPWorldTour

videos.title: Sidney Crosby's Playbook: The Mechanic Troy Terry Mastered
videos.summary_source_text:
Sidney Crosby's Playbook: The Mechanic Troy Terry Mastered Troy Terry has mastered two key strategies for attacking triangles and exposing defenders and I uncovered them by accident when Pavel Barber and I put GoPros on our heads during 3-on-3s.

What I realized re-watching that footage: there's way more time on the puck than I thought, if you pay attention to where defenders' sticks are positioned. And if you bait your opponent correctly, you can actually use their stick momentum against them. That's when I started dissecting players like Crosby, I found Troy Terry has perfected this dangle by design movement.

In this video I break down:
→ Strategy 1- Using stick momentum against defenders by positioning the puck to bait the swing.
→ How Terry creates open space by getting defenders to commit their stick to the wrong area.
→ Strategy 2- The anchor mechanic Crosby uses (and Terry perfected) to precisely place the puck.
→ Why dragging your foot creates more lateral movement with less effort than pushing.
→ The Crosby curl, the shuffle, and the cross-unders that set up these triangle attacks.

These aren't just hockey IQ moves they're built on specific mechanics. The anchor, the corkscrew, the lateral movements that let you place the puck exactly where the defender can't reach.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

videos.title: Så går det till på en Kinesisk provkörning!
videos.summary_source_text:
Så går det till på en Kinesisk provkörning! Jag är på plats i Kina för att köra märket som varit landets största bilexportör i 23 år – Chery! Med siktet inställt på Sverige visar de upp allt från små elbilen QQ3 till flaggskeppet Tiggo 9 "Super Hybrid". Bra teknik och byggkvalitet som utmanar de etablerade märkena, till priser som får en svensk tjänstebilsförare att gnugga ögonen. 
Men hur är de att köra? Får jag köra? Jag har aldrig kört så lite bil på en så lång provkörningsresa. Och får tjata mig till att köra Tiggo 9. Det var slupen som abvgjorde vilen av de fem bilarna som fanns på plats, som jag fick köra. Och att byta bil längs provkörningssträckan, nej det gick inte!
Eventet är mer för att dokumentera att vi varit där än att vi får köra bilarna. Och vad är grejen med att de livesänder allt på Facebook med "tre tittare"? 
Följ med på den knasigaste provkörning jag gjort! Mer roadmovie, än provkörning om sanningen ska fram! Genom kinesiska Tier 3-städer, bröllopsfester och autonom parkering!

videos.title: The Hard Work Paradox In Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
The Hard Work Paradox In Hockey The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: The Real Reason You're Bloated After Eating
videos.summary_source_text:
The Real Reason You're Bloated After Eating Why am I bloated after eating? Bloating after eating is often linked to poor digestive health, not just the food you ate. Learn how to relieve stomach bloating and improve digestion naturally by addressing the root cause.

🌟 Which of the 10 health signals are you missing? Take the 2-minute quiz to discover your #1 Health Lever: https://drbrg.co/3Rfk5FP

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

Fix your gut with one microbe: ▶️ https://youtu.be/nZV1oYv5Ddo

How to make L. Reuteri yogurt: ➡️ https://drbrg.co/4wokndO
[Affiliate links]
Yogurt maker: https://amzn.to/4rYCu6w
Yogurt jars: https://amzn.to/411hSPw 
Yogurt containers: https://amzn.to/411eXX0

0:00 Introduction: Why you feel bloated after eating
1:19 Digestive health and belly bloating 
3:45 The gallbladder and the pancreas
5:27 SIBO and the gut microbiome 
8:58 Stomach bloating location explained
10:52 Low stomach acid and belly bloating 
16:26 Bloating remedies

Keep watching to discover the best ways to improve gut health and eliminate bloating naturally:
▶️ https://youtu.be/jZ4jNALF9Ys
▶️ https://youtu.be/RCjQAQuWiYc
▶️ https://youtu.be/zXd86RvNzZA

Low stomach acid is a common problem and may be the reason you feel bloated after eating. Healthy stomach acid is essential for proper digestion and helps trigger the release of bile, which is critical for breaking down fats and absorbing nutrients.

Bile is produced by the liver and stored in the gallbladder. When bile flow or gallbladder function is impaired, digestion can suffer and place additional stress on the pancreas.

The pancreas produces insulin as well as digestive enzymes needed to break down food properly. Poor digestion can contribute to stomach bloating, discomfort, and nutrient absorption issues.

Small intestinal bacterial overgrowth (SIBO) is another common cause of bloating after meals. Poor gut health and sluggish digestion can also lead to constipation and ongoing digestive problems.

Even the specific location of your stomach bloating may provide clues about the root cause of your digestive issues.

Low stomach acid is a common problem and may be the reason you feel bloated after eating. Healthy stomach acid is essential for proper digestion and helps trigger the release of bile, which is critical for breaking down fats and absorbing nutrients.

Bile is produced by the liver and stored in the gallbladder. When bile flow or gallbladder function is impaired, digestion can suffer and place additional stress on the pancreas.

The pancreas produces insulin as well as digestive enzymes needed to break down food properly. Poor digestion can contribute to stomach bloating, discomfort, and nutrient absorption issues.

Small intestinal bacterial overgrowth (SIBO) is another common cause of bloating after meals. Poor gut health and sluggish digestion can also lead to constipation and ongoing digestive problems.

Even the specific location of your stomach bloating may provide clues about the root cause of your digestive issues.

videos.title: This Vitamin Deficiency May Be Triggering Your Dizziness
videos.summary_source_text:
This Vitamin Deficiency May Be Triggering Your Dizziness Can you guess the #1 vitamin deficiency behind vertigo? 

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

DATA:
https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00405-019-05694-0
https://vitamindwiki.com/tiki-index.php?page_id=3460#BPPV_eliminated_by_Vitamin_D_April_2019
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23096068/

0:00 Introduction: BPPV explained 
0:38 What causes vertigo? 
1:15 The Dix-Hallpike test
3:00 The Epley maneuver
4:48 What causes calcium crystals? 
7:35 The best remedy for BPPV
9:00 Learn more about vitamin D deficiency! 

Today I want to cover a very specific type of vertigo called benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (BPPV). 

This type of vertigo makes you feel like you’re spinning when you’re not, and it’s the #1 cause of dizziness. 

This condition is typically caused by a calcium crystal that has broken off and is in the canals of the inner ear that control equilibrium and balance. 

There is a simple test called the Dix-Hallpike test that you can do at home to help determine if you have this type of vertigo. 

Check with your doctor before doing this test to ensure you don’t have an issue with your cervical spine that could prevent you from rotating your neck. 

After you perform the test and determine which side is causing a problem, you can try the Epley maneuver. The goal of this maneuver is to create different motions to slowly move the calcium crystal out of the ear.

An interesting systematic review and meta-analysis done in 2020 showed an association between low vitamin D and BPPV. 

Vitamin D supports the absorption and transportation of calcium. It's important to note that some people have a genetic issue that causes normal vitamin D levels in the blood but not the tissues. 

I believe one of the best natural remedies for BPPV is a combination of vitamin D3 and vitamin K2. For every 10,000 IU of vitamin D3, you would want 100 mcg of vitamin K2. Talk to your doctor about taking 50,000 IU of vitamin D3 and 500 mcg of vitamin K2 once a week for BPPV.

When using high doses of vitamin D, always check with your doctor. High doses of vitamin D can increase blood/urine calcium levels resulting in calcium deposits in the kidney. Also drink 2.5 liters of fluids per day, take cofactors with vitamin D (magnesium, K2 and zinc) and have your doctor monitor your PTH, vitamin D blood levels and creatinine and avoid excess calcium supplements of high calcium foods when taking vitamin D levels over 10,000 IUs.

videos.title: 9.5: Wallstedt dazzles with 35-save night, ties Roloson in Wild history books 🇸🇪⭐
videos.summary_source_text:
9.5: Wallstedt dazzles with 35-save night, ties Roloson in Wild history books 🇸🇪⭐ Don't call it a comeback! Jesper Wallstedt is simply sensational in Game 3, stopping 35 of 36 shots the Avalanche send his way to help lead his Wild to a huge 5-1 victory - their first of the series. The win is Wallstedt's 5th of these playoffs, tying him with Dwayne Roloson (5 wins in 2003) for the most in a single postseason in Wild history! Wallstedt's incredible night in the crease earns him both 3rd star of the game and Top European Player of the Night honors!

videos.title: Minnesota Controls the Action, Gets Back Into Series With a Game 3 Win Over the Avalanche
videos.summary_source_text:
Minnesota Controls the Action, Gets Back Into Series With a Game 3 Win Over the Avalanche Minnesota Wild overpower Colorado Avalanche with relentless play and tactical precision. After dropping their first game in seven playoff outings, the Avalanche face pressing questions about their offensive cohesion and ability to adjust. Zach Pearson and Chris Micieli break down how Kirill Kaprizov’s opportunistic goal, Ryan Hartman’s power play presence, and Brock Faber’s net-front drive set the tone for the Wild’s 5-1 victory. Was Colorado’s signature double drop pass power play strategy finally exposed, and can Jared Bednar’s squad recover?

The hosts examine Nathan MacKinnon’s frustration, Scott Wedgewood’s short night in goal, and the Avalanche’s ongoing battle with zone entry and board battles. They debate whether Mackenzie Blackwood should start next, highlight Minnesota’s defensive lockdown, and discuss controversial calls involving Quinn Hughes. How will Colorado respond after a rare playoff letdown? Don’t miss this spirited, stat-packed analysis of the Avalanche’s quest to regain control of their Stanley Cup run.

After dropping their first game in seven playoff outings, the Avalanche face pressing questions about their offensive cohesion and ability to adjust. Zach Pearson and Chris Micieli break down how Kirill Kaprizov’s opportunistic goal, Ryan Hartman’s power play presence, and Brock Faber’s net-front drive set the tone for the Wild’s 5-1 victory. Was Colorado’s signature double drop pass power play strategy finally exposed, and can Jared Bednar’s squad recover?

videos.title: PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 3 | Truist Championship | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 3 | Truist Championship | 2026 Check out the best shots of the day from Round 3 of the 2026 Truist Championship, featuring Alex Fitzpatrick, Kristoffer Reitan, Cameron Young, and Sungjae Im, among others. 

The PGA TOUR heads to Quail Hollow Club in Charlotte, North Carolina, for the Truist Championship, one of the standout events on the calendar. Rory McIlroy returns to a venue where he has enjoyed incredible success and headlines a star-studded field. He is joined by a host of top talent including Cameron Young, Xander Schauffele, Justin Thomas, Tommy Fleetwood, Matt Fitzpatrick and Justin Rose, as players continue their push for FedExCup points during a crucial stretch of the season. Known for its demanding closing stretch, the “Green Mile,” Quail Hollow promises drama and excitement down the back nine. With elite talent, iconic holes, and high stakes, expect plenty of standout performances throughout the week.

videos.title: Anton Lundell Talks Missing Playoffs for First Time with Florida Panthers
videos.summary_source_text:
Anton Lundell Talks Missing Playoffs for First Time with Florida Panthers Florida Panthers center Anton Lundell meets the media after his team wrapped up its season at the IcePlex in Fort Lauderdale on April 16, 2026. 

This is the first time in Lundell’s five seasons the Panthers missed the playoffs.

Lundell has joined captain Sasha Barkov on Team Finland for the IIHF World Championship.

The Panthers won three straight to end the season but missed the Stanley Cup Playoffs. 

Florida won the past two Stanley Cup championships. 

The two-time Stanley Cup champions will miss the playoffs for the first time since 2019. 

Lundell was a first-round pick by Florida in 2020 and made his debut with the team in 2021.

Video Courtesy Florida Panthers / @FlaPanthers / Panthers Vision

Anthropic Situation Just Got Even More INSANE Anthropic just entered one of the strangest moments in AI. Claude is suddenly tied to SpaceX compute, Google Cloud, Amazon, Microsoft, Nvidia, the Pentagon, and a secret cybersecurity model called Mythos. The company once known as the careful OpenAI rival may now be turning into one of the biggest players in the real AI infrastructure war.

How Anthropic’s SpaceX deal gives Claude access to Colossus One compute

videos.title: Your mental swing path is not real…(Righthanded)
videos.channel: Better & Easier golf with your guide Marcus Edblad
videos.summary_source_text:
Your mental swing path is not real…(Righthanded) The rotation or turn of our body will make you feel one thing but execute another. Almost giving up I started to maximize the out feeling and found the solution. #golftips #pga #golfswing 

0:00 Intro to the mental and real swing path

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Let me help you get an effortless golfswing with speed, distance and accuracy. I call it the circle of speed. A good release will help you to a better impact and longer drives and irons without slice and hook. How to swing a golf club need to be adjusted to your skill level and experience. 

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating. 

We also need to understand that many myths in golf are misleading. Everything that you find on the internet isn’t true. Ben Hogan, Sam Snead are two of the legends that are often misunderstood.  To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out. 

A will never call a slice sexy but a high draw is beautiful in a very special way. Some pro's that hit draw: Rory McIroy, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, Sepp Straka, Bryson Dechambeau  and many, many more. 

We shall not keep our head still, only start with the hips, look only at the ball or keep the left or right arm straight. Understand what do do with the club and let your body find the best way possible. 

This is not Rick Shiels, Saguto golf, Danny Maude, Meandmygolf, the Average Golfer, Malaska, Mark Crossfield, Alex Elliot Golf or Paige Spiranac. This is a method that anyone can learn if you give it a chance. 

If you use callaway, ping, titleist, takomo or taylor made clubs doesn’t matter you can learn to hit good golf shots. Your loft or your shaft on the club will not change but you can always get better. 

Find what inspires you. Golf basics are the same for everyone. The pga tour, longdrivers or other good golfers can all help you find better golf shots. Watch what they do instead of listed to what they say. Listen mostly to your body and figure out what you need to do to produce the same results as the pros on TV. Be careful when you watch a golf swing in slow motion. The result isn't always what the golfer is trying to do. Feel and real are seldom the same. 

We can find inspiration in a lot of things. You can find training motivation watching movies like Rocky, Tin Cup or Happy Gilmore and find mental inspiration in legends like Arnold Schwarzenegger, Hulk Hogan, Sylvester Stallone or Mike Tyson. Maybe we sometimes need to hear about crazy achievements from people like David Goggins, Jocko Willink, Ct Fletcher, Jim Rohn or Tony Robbins. 

Mike Malaska, Mike Austin, Mike Dunaway, Pete Cowen and Steve Pratt are some of my favorite golf instructors online. Find the tips that make your golf better. Decide what you want to hit. I teach a draw but many like a fade and nobody likes a hook or slice. 

Your hips or your lag is not the key to golf. Your hands will decide the shot and you can do like Tiger Woods and trust your hands. Teach your hands to hit different shots. Hit big hooks and slices to understand what happens. Only straight is not gonna teach you anything. 

Bryson Dechambeu and Kyle Birkshire can inspire you to hit it longer. Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson can give you inspiration and motivation to test new things and new golf shots. Linn Grant, Viktor Hovland and Ludvig Aberg can show you the future of golf. 

The Masters, Players Championship, The Players, The Open and US Open can make you love golf even more. See the best in the world solve all the problems we all need to master any golf course. Golf is a craft and you need fantasy, feel and skills to master it.

Questions my videos will answer:

How to swing faster. How to hit longer. How to swing faster. How to hit your irons better. How to be better at anything. How to master the circle of speed. How to get a better short game. How to hit your wedge better. How to release the golf club. How to rotate in golf. Best tips for beginner golfers. How to hit down on the ball. How to hit up on the ball. How to rotate hips in the golf swing. How to hit straight every time. How to stop slicing. How to swing effortlessly. 

#subscribe #golftips #pga  #hurricane 


Filmed with Iphone 15 pro or Iphone 17 The rotation or turn of our body will make you feel one thing but execute another. Almost giving up I started to maximize the out feeling and found the solution. #golftips #pga #golfswing

To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out. 

A will never call a slice sexy but a high draw is beautiful in a very special way. Some pro's that hit draw: Rory McIroy, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, Sepp Straka, Bryson Dechambeau  and many, many more. 

We shall not keep our head still, only start with the hips, look only at the ball or keep the left or right arm straight. Understand what do do with the club and let your body find the best way possible. 

This is not Rick Shiels, Saguto golf, Danny Maude, Meandmygolf, the Average Golfer, Malaska, Mark Crossfield, Alex Elliot Golf or Paige Spiranac. This is a method that anyone can learn if you give it a chance. 

If you use callaway, ping, titleist, takomo or taylor made clubs doesn’t matter you can learn to hit good golf shots. Your loft or your shaft on the club will not change but you can always get better. 

Find what inspires you. Golf basics are the same for everyone. The pga tour, longdrivers or other good golfers can all help you find better golf shots. Watch what they do instead of listed to what they say. Listen mostly to your body and figure out what you need to do to produce the same results as the pros on TV. Be careful when you watch a golf swing in slow motion. The result isn't always what the golfer is trying to do. Feel and real are seldom the same. 

We can find inspiration in a lot of things. You can find training motivation watching movies like Rocky, Tin Cup or Happy Gilmore and find mental inspiration in legends like Arnold Schwarzenegger, Hulk Hogan, Sylvester Stallone or Mike Tyson. Maybe we sometimes need to hear about crazy achievements from people like David Goggins, Jocko Willink, Ct Fletcher, Jim Rohn or Tony Robbins. 

Mike Malaska, Mike Austin, Mike Dunaway, Pete Cowen and Steve Pratt are some of my favorite golf instructors online. Find the tips that make your golf better. Decide what you want to hit. I teach a draw but many like a fade and nobody likes a hook or slice. 

Your hips or your lag is not the key to golf. Your hands will decide the shot and you can do like Tiger Woods and trust your hands. Teach your hands to hit different shots. Hit big hooks and slices to understand what happens. Only straight is not gonna teach you anything. 

Bryson Dechambeu and Kyle Birkshire can inspire you to hit it longer. Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson can give you inspiration and motivation to test new things and new golf shots. Linn Grant, Viktor Hovland and Ludvig Aberg can show you the future of golf. 

The Masters, Players Championship, The Players, The Open and US Open can make you love golf even more. See the best in the world solve all the problems we all need to master any golf course. Golf is a craft and you need fantasy, feel and skills to master it.

Questions my videos will answer:

How to swing faster. How to hit longer. How to swing faster. How to hit your irons better. How to be better at anything. How to master the circle of speed. How to get a better short game. How to hit your wedge better. How to release the golf club. How to rotate in golf. Best tips for beginner golfers. How to hit down on the ball. How to hit up on the ball. How to rotate hips in the golf swing. How to hit straight every time. How to stop slicing. How to swing effortlessly.

videos.title: Det mentala och det verkliga svingspåret…
videos.channel: Bättre & Enklare golf med Marcus Edblad PGA
videos.summary_source_text:
Det mentala och det verkliga svingspåret… Om vi vill ändra svingspår behöver vi förstå hur rotationen påverkar oss. För att svinga mer utåt behöver vi överdriva otroligt mycket för att få det vi vill. #golf #golfswing #golftips 


Nu finns det möjlighet att genom appen Skillest göra mig till din personliga tränare. Du hittar mig på denna länk: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Låt mig hjälpa dig finna bättre slag, bollträff, riktning och svinghastighet. Oavsett om du slår en wedge, järnklubba eller driver kan du med dina händer lära dig golfslag som du inte trodde var möjliga. Längre och rakare slag kräver alltid träning men något alla kan lära sig.  

Vare sig du fightas med en slice eller en hook så har jag videos som kan guida till bättre golf. Är du nybörjare så kan du börja med min grönt kort eller nybörjarkurs.

Försök ersätta dåliga vanor med nya bättre vanor. Oavsett vilket loft eller skaft din klubba har så är det du som bestämmer vad som ska hända. Oavsett om det står callaway, ping, titleist eller taylor made på klubban så kan du slå bra slag.

Var noga med vad du tittar på. Det finns många duktiga instruktörer på nätet, några av mina favoriter är Mike Malaska, Mike Dunaway, Mike Austin.

Försök fokusera på grunderna i slaget bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår. Träna med fördel på en sak i taget. Tränar du din svinghastighet måste du tillåta att det går snett och tränar du raka slag kan du inte samtidigt svinga i max fart.

Ha tålamod och låt det ta sin tid, luras inte av genvägar som bara gör att du tappar fokus från det viktiga. Golf kommer alltid vara svårt men vi kan med träning se till att vi blir bättre för varje dag som går.

Titta mycket på golf på TV. Bryson Dechambeu, Dustin Johnson, Rory Mcilroy kan inspirera till längre slag och andra som Tiger Woods, Viktor Hovland, Ludvig Åberg och Phil Mickelson kan ge dig fantasi att skapa nya slag.

Träna styrketräning för hela kroppen för att må bra genom livet och skapa en effektiv maskin. Träna händer, handleder och armar för att skapa en effektiv release och mer svinghastighet.

Du kan finna inspiration i filmer som Happy Gilmore, Tin Cup eller Bagger Vance. Filmer som Rocky kan ge dig motivation att ta dig till gymet. Legender som Arnold Schwarzenegger, Sylvester Stallone eller Hulk Hogan kan få dig att vilja bli starkare och Moe Norman, Arnold Palmer, Ben Hogan, Sam Snead eller Jack Nicklaus kan inspirera dig att bli en bättre golfare. 

Ge aldrig upp i sökandet och träningen för att få bästa bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår.

Filmat med iPhone 17 och redigerat i iMovie på en Macbook. Om vi vill ändra svingspår behöver vi förstå hur rotationen påverkar oss. För att svinga mer utåt behöver vi överdriva otroligt mycket för att få det vi vill. #golf #golfswing #golftips 


Nu finns det möjlighet att genom appen Skillest göra mig till din personliga tränare. Du hittar mig på denna länk: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Låt mig hjälpa dig finna bättre slag, bollträff, riktning och svinghastighet. Oavsett om du slår en wedge, järnklubba eller driver kan du med dina händer lära dig golfslag som du inte trodde var möjliga. Längre och rakare slag kräver alltid träning men något alla kan lära sig.  

Vare sig du fightas med en slice eller en hook så har jag videos som kan guida till bättre golf. Är du nybörjare så kan du börja med min grönt kort eller nybörjarkurs.

Försök ersätta dåliga vanor med nya bättre vanor. Oavsett vilket loft eller skaft din klubba har så är det du som bestämmer vad som ska hända. Oavsett om det står callaway, ping, titleist eller taylor made på klubban så kan du slå bra slag.

Var noga med vad du tittar på. Det finns många duktiga instruktörer på nätet, några av mina favoriter är Mike Malaska, Mike Dunaway, Mike Austin.

Försök fokusera på grunderna i slaget bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår. Träna med fördel på en sak i taget. Tränar du din svinghastighet måste du tillåta att det går snett och tränar du raka slag kan du inte samtidigt svinga i max fart.

Ha tålamod och låt det ta sin tid, luras inte av genvägar som bara gör att du tappar fokus från det viktiga. Golf kommer alltid vara svårt men vi kan med träning se till att vi blir bättre för varje dag som går.

Titta mycket på golf på TV. Bryson Dechambeu, Dustin Johnson, Rory Mcilroy kan inspirera till längre slag och andra som Tiger Woods, Viktor Hovland, Ludvig Åberg och Phil Mickelson kan ge dig fantasi att skapa nya slag.

Träna styrketräning för hela kroppen för att må bra genom livet och skapa en effektiv maskin. Träna händer, handleder och armar för att skapa en effektiv release och mer svinghastighet.

Du kan finna inspiration i filmer som Happy Gilmore, Tin Cup eller Bagger Vance. Filmer som Rocky kan ge dig motivation att ta dig till gymet. Legender som Arnold Schwarzenegger, Sylvester Stallone eller Hulk Hogan kan få dig att vilja bli starkare och Moe Norman, Arnold Palmer, Ben Hogan, Sam Snead eller Jack Nicklaus kan inspirera dig att bli en bättre golfare. 

Ge aldrig upp i sökandet och träningen för att få bästa bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår.

Filmat med iPhone 17 och redigerat i iMovie på en Macbook.

videos.summary_source_text:
I Was Coached Wrong for Years. So I Spent 10 Years Changing Hockey Coaching I Was Coached Wrong for Years. So I Spent 10 Years Changing Hockey Coaching.

Train 2.0 didn't start as a business plan. It started as frustration. I kept asking 
my coaches why. Why this drill, why this technique, why this way  and the answers didn't hold up. So I went headfirst into the science of how elite players actually move and began my undergrade in Kinesiology at UBC. 
 
What I found was a complete disconnect between what the NHL's best were 
doing and what traditional coaching was teaching.

videos.title: Round 3 Highlights | 2026 Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship
videos.summary_source_text:
Round 3 Highlights | 2026 Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship Highlights from round three of the Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship at Real Club de Golf El Prat in Barcelona.

► SUBSCRIBE: http://et.golf/Subscribe 

The DP World Tour is back to where it all started this week. Spain was the host of the first ever DP World Tour - then European Tour - event in 1972 so it is fitting that it is where the 2026 European Swing gets started. The fourth of five Global Swings on the 2026 Race to Dubai, the European Swing consists of six counting events - four of which are national opens - with the US PGA Championship and U.S. Open also happening within the same timeframe. Spain has long been one of the world's great golfing nations and this week local heroes will take centre stage, with a string of established and rising stars searching for home glory. Nacho Elvira has already won in 2026 and will be hungry for more, as will fellow winners in recent seasons Eugenio Chacarra, Alejandro del Rey, Angel Hidalgo and Pablo Larrazábal.

Welcome to the Official YouTube channel of the DP World Tour. Every week you can find extended tournament highlights and individual player highlights from the likes of Rory McIlroy, Justin Rose and Tommy Fleetwood. This is also the home of the 14 Club Challenge, Little Interviews, Fastest Hole and many more challenges, pranks and world record attempts featuring the biggest golfers in the world.

videos.title: Stop Shooting Like It's 1992. Here's How Modern Snipers Do It.
videos.summary_source_text:
Stop Shooting Like It's 1992. Here's How Modern Snipers Do It. Iginla and Sakic ushered in the next evolution of shooting in hockey and decades later, most players are still being taught like it's 1987.

The science is clear. Modern sticks are engineered to flex, and the players who finish at a high level are the ones who let the stick do the work. Rotate the body, let the puck lag behind, whip the stick through, and the flex generates power you can't create through muscle alone. It's a mechanical advantage, not a strength contest.

But step into most rinks and you'll still see kids being coached to wind up and whack the puck like they're swinging an axe. 

We reverse-engineered what modern snipers like Bedard, Matthews and Pettersson do, built the Slingshot Shooting System around it, and the result is a shot that takes less effort and produces more velocity for any player willing to trade muscling through for moving correctly.

videos.title: Before Hitting Your Woods Just DO THIS For 5 Seconds
videos.summary_source_text:
Before Hitting Your Woods Just DO THIS For 5 Seconds Struggling to make consistent contact with your fairway woods and hybrids? You're not alone — and you're not doing anything drastically wrong. These are the longest clubs in your bag with the least amount of loft, so be easy on yourself. But there is something you can feel before every single shot that will transform how consistently you strike them.

Data from Shot Scope tells us that mid to high handicap golfers use fairway woods and hybrids 25% of the time. That's a quarter of every round. Improve your consistency with these clubs and the effect on your score is enormous. In this fairway wood lesson I want to show you exactly what I showed my students John and Tom this week — two simple things that control everything about how your hybrid and fairway wood strikes the ground, delivers loft and gets the ball flying high and long.

The first is about controlling the depth of your arc — how the club interacts with the ground rather than where it lands. Get this right and the topping, the fatting and the inconsistent contact start to disappear almost immediately. videos.title: Reset Your Body in 14 Days (No Sugar)
videos.summary_source_text:
Reset Your Body in 14 Days (No Sugar) What happens when you stop eating sugar for 14 days? Most people quit sugar right before the benefits begin. Discover how a sugar detox affects your body and can transform your health.

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements

🌟 Which of the 10 health signals are you missing? Take the 2-minute quiz to discover your #1 Health Lever: https://drbrg.co/4uCDM90

0:00 Introduction: What happens when you stop eating sugar? 
0:20 Why you should stop eating sugar
0:40 Insulin resistance
1:00 Sugar detox day 1
5:20 The benefits of quitting sugar day 5 
7:42 How to continue healthy eating after cutting out sugar 

Keep watching to learn how to break sugar cravings, avoid hidden sugars, and support healthy weight loss naturally:
▶️ https://youtu.be/iG67l-wqOV8
▶️ https://youtu.be/LoKAhLfPUYA
▶️ https://youtu.be/G1Z4703YstY

Learn how to read and understand food labels with this free guide: https://drbrg.co/4wk8ENg

Keto Recipes: https://www.youtube.com/@drbergketorecipes 

Most people who try to quit sugar fail around day 3 or 4. This is often when the brain’s reward system is at its lowest point, causing many people to give up right before things start to improve.

Sugar can combine with proteins in the body through a process called glycation, permanently damaging tissues and nerve endings. Over time, a high-sugar diet can also contribute to insulin resistance.

When quitting sugar, it’s important to eliminate hidden sugars as well. Starches are a common hidden sugar found in many ultra-processed foods.

Brain fog, irritability, and fatigue are common during a sugar detox and often drive people back to sugar. This happens because the brain is craving dopamine and pushing you to consume more sugar.

Around day 5, many people begin experiencing the benefits of quitting sugar, including reduced cravings, lower inflammation, improved mental clarity, better energy, and weight loss.

B vitamins, potassium, and magnesium may also help reduce sugar cravings and support the body during the transition.

Dr. Eric Berg DC Bio:
Dr. Berg, age 61, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals and author of the best-selling book The Healthy Keto Plan. He no longer practices, but focuses on health education through social media.

Dr. Berg is a licensed chiropractor in Virginia, California, and Louisiana, but he no longer practices chiropractic in any state and does not see patients, so he can focus on educating people as a full-time activity, yet he maintains an active license. This video is for general informational purposes only. It should not be used to self-diagnose, and it is not a substitute for a medical exam, cure, treatment, diagnosis, prescription, or recommendation. It does not create a doctor-patient relationship between Dr. Berg and you. You should not make any change in your health regimen or diet before first consulting a physician and obtaining a medical exam, diagnosis, and recommendation. Always seek the advice of a physician or other qualified health provider with any questions you may have regarding a medical condition.
